channel.title: Dataset on Adventures in Data Science
channel.description: Recent content in Dataset on Adventures in Data Science

item.title: Lead and Chicago
item.description: ## Warning: Duplicated column names deduplicated: 'Neighborhood' ## => 'Neighborhood_1' [3], 'Neighborhood' => 'Neighborhood_2' [4], ## 'Neighborhood' => 'Neighborhood_3' [5], 'Neighborhood' => ## 'Neighborhood_4' [6], 'Neighborhood' => 'Neighborhood_5' [7], ## 'Neighborhood' => 'Neighborhood_6' [8], 'Neighborhood' => ## 'Neighborhood_7' [9], 'Neighborhood' => 'Neighborhood_8' [10] I am aware that there is a huge outlier in this data set 1, but I needed to push this data set out to win an argument at work.

item.title: Mental Health in Tech: Early EDA
item.description: As much fun as reading and online coursework is, at the end of the day, I need to start putting these skills to use. Data set’s like Iris or mtcars, while super fun for training, do not really hold the same place in my heart like data sets involving issues that I deal with at work. Enter Kaggle. I thought it was mostly about competitions. As it so often happens, I was wrong.
